---
title: Submit data to BigPicture
subtitle: An operator's manual — from a bare machine to a validated upload
---

This is a **step-by-step manual** for submitting a dataset to BigPicture. It is
written for a **technically proficient user who is not a software developer**:
someone comfortable installing software and running commands in a terminal, but
who is **not** expected to know Python, Docker internals, or the command line in
depth.

Follow it **top to bottom**. Every step tells you exactly what to paste and what
you should see back, and the **« Prev / Next »** buttons at the bottom of each
page walk you through the ten submission steps (A–J), in order, from a bare
machine to a validated upload. You never have to guess what to do next.

::: {.callout-important}
## Before you start: you need access

This manual assumes you have **already been granted access** to the private
BigPicture repositories and container images used by the submission pipeline. If
you have not, the very first steps will fail when you try to download the code.

Request access **before** you begin — see [Prerequisites](prerequisites.qmd) and
[GitHub access](github-access.qmd).
:::

## The journey at a glance

You will move through ten steps, **A** to **J**. Each builds on the one before,
so do them in order the first time.

| | Step | What you'll do |
|---|---|---|
| | [**Key concepts**](key-concepts.qmd) | The handful of words you need: *model*, *mappers*, *metadata*, *bundle*. |
| **A** | [Prerequisites](prerequisites.qmd) | Install the tools (Docker, Git, `gh`, `make`, OpenSSL, Python) and verify each. |
| **B** | [GitHub access](github-access.qmd) | Sign in with `gh` so you can reach the private repos and images. |
| **C** | [Get the code](get-the-code.qmd) | Clone the pipeline (with submodules) and pull the images. |
| **D** | [Configure](configure.qmd) | Set up `.env`, mount your data, and set the config salt. |
| **E** | [Reference data](reference-data.qmd) | Provide the `model.json` and `mappers.xlsx` for your site. |
| **F** | [TLS + credentials](tls-credentials.qmd) | Generate certificates and login credentials. |
| **G** | [Preflight + boot](boot.qmd) | Run the doctor check, start the stack, open the UI. |
| **H** | [Run a submission](run-submission.qmd) | Log in, create a project/batch, upload your workbook, curate, export. |
| **I** | [Validate the bundle](validate.qmd) | Check the export and learn how to read the errors. |
| **J** | [Upload to BigPicture](upload.qmd) | Push the validated bundle with `bp-submitter`. |

## How to read this manual

- **Three operating systems, one manual.** Where a command differs, you'll see
  **Windows / macOS / Linux** tabs — pick your tab and ignore the rest. See the
  [page template](contributing/page-template.qmd) for the conventions.
- **Verify as you go.** Most steps end with a *Verify* box: paste the command,
  compare against the expected output, and only move on once it matches.
- **Linux is the reference path.** Every command-bearing step is validated on a
  clean Linux machine; Windows and macOS tabs follow the same shape.

## Need help?

- **Data submission:** [bp-ops](mailto:{{< var email.helpdesk >}})
- **Support:** [servicedesk](mailto:{{< var email.servicedesk >}})
